name: Excessive Blocked Connections from Single Source (SIEM Correlation Needed)
description: Detects an excessive number of network connection attempts from the same source IP that have been explicitly blocked or denied by a firewall or IDS/IPS within a short timeframe. This indicates malicious activity such as port scanning, vulnerability scanning, brute-force attacks, or Denial of Service (DoS) reconnaissance.

parameters.name: Timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of one of the blocked connection events (e.g., the most recent one).

parameters.name: SourceIp
parameters.description: The source IP address attempting the connection.

parameters.name: DestinationIp
parameters.description: The destination IP address being targeted.

parameters.name: Action
parameters.description: The action taken by the firewall/IDS (expected 'blocked', 'deny', or 'drop').

parameters.name: RuleId
parameters.description: The ID of the specific firewall rule that blocked the connection, if available.

parameters.name: DeviceName
parameters.description: The name of the firewall/IDS device reporting the event, if available.

parameters.name: RuleName
parameters.description: The name of the Sigma rule that triggered the event, if applicable.

parameters.name: SIEM_COUNT_OF_EVENTS
parameters.description: The aggregated count of blocked connection events by the SIEM (e.g., 75).

parameters.name: SIEM_TIMEFRAME
parameters.description: The time window over which the events were counted (e.g., '1 minute').

parameters.name: SIEM_FIRST_EVENT_TIMESTAMP
parameters.description: The timestamp of the first event in the correlated group.

parameters.name: SIEM_LAST_EVENT_TIMESTAMP
parameters.description: The timestamp of the last event in the correlated group.
